food_subgroup: Leavened flatbread with cheese and garlic topping

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general naan bread class (FOODON_03302051); no specific garlic-and-cheese naan subclass exists. closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated garlic-cheese naan code; A00LC (flatbread) is the nearest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for garlic and cheese naan or naan bread specifically; none assigned.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard garlic and cheese naan piece weighing approximately 120 g, as typically served in an Indian restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Naan bread, plain — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18960); adapted with cheese and garlic additions estimated from USDA Foundation Foods (Mozzarella cheese NDB 01026, Garlic raw NDB 11215) and ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (Naan, plain)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / ICMR-National Institute of Nutrition
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for garlic-and-cheese naan. Values are derived by adapting plain naan nutrient data from USDA SR Legacy (NDB 18960, ~272 kcal/100g plain naan) and ICMR-NIN plain naan data, then proportionally adjusting for approximately 10–12% cheese addition (mozzarella, USDA NDB 01026) and ~3% garlic (USDA NDB 11215) by weight. The resulting composite reflects a typical restaurant-style garlic cheese naan. Energy and macros are cross-checked against ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017 edition) plain naan baseline.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; naan bread GI reference; plain white-flour naan GI ~71 (University of Sydney GI Database)
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database lists plain naan bread (white flour) with a GI of approximately 71 (high). Garlic and cheese additions do not substantially lower the GI of the bread base; the fat and protein from cheese may marginally reduce postprandial glucose response, but the dominant carbohydrate matrix remains refined white flour. GI of 71 is retained as a conservative and defensible estimate for garlic-and-cheese naan. GL per serving calculated as: (71 × 47.5 × 1.20) / 100 = 40.5 g per 120 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~420 mg/100g; ~504 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit garlic and cheese naan to occasional consumption, as one piece provides approximately 504 mg sodium — about 22% of the 2,300 mg daily limit — primarily from cheese and added salt; choosing a smaller portion or requesting reduced-salt preparation is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 71) and high glycemic load (~40 per serving) from refined white flour base
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat garlic and cheese naan as an occasional food; its high GI (71) and high glycemic load (~40 per 120 g serving) from refined maida flour can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation, and portion should be strictly limited to half a piece paired with high-fibre accompaniments such as dal or vegetable curry.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from cheese and butter (~3.8 g/100g) and moderate sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, garlic and cheese naan should be consumed sparingly; the saturated fat contribution from cheese and ghee (~4.6 g per serving) combined with moderate sodium (~504 mg per serving) can adversely affect LDL cholesterol and blood pressure when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Garlic and Cheese Naan is a popular Indian leavened flatbread made from refined white wheat flour (maida) enriched with yogurt, yeast or baking powder, and butter or ghee, then topped or stuffed with minced garlic and melted cheese — typically processed cheese or mozzarella — before being baked in a tandoor (clay oven) at high heat. The result is a soft, slightly chewy bread with a golden, blistered surface, a rich buttery aroma, and a savoury garlic-cheese flavour. It is commonly served as an accompaniment to Indian curries, dal, or grilled meats, and is a staple offering at Indian restaurants across Singapore and the broader South and Southeast Asian diaspora. Nutritionally, garlic and cheese naan is energy-dense (~295 kcal/100g), with a significant carbohydrate load (~47.5 g/100g) from refined flour, moderate protein (~9.8 g/100g) boosted by cheese, and moderate fat (~7.2 g/100g) including saturated fat from dairy. Its high glycemic index (~71) makes it a food of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. The sodium content (~420 mg/100g) is relevant for those with hypertension. Calcium from cheese (~115 mg/100g) is a positive micronutrient contribution. Clinically, this dish is best consumed in moderation and paired with fibre-rich accompaniments to blunt glycemic impact.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a garlic cheese naan?
Can diabetics eat garlic cheese naan?
What is the GI of naan bread?
How much sodium is in cheese naan?

ingredient_synonyms.paneer: Indian cottage cheese (not used here, but common confusion)
ingredient_synonyms.tandoor: clay oven used for baking naan

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for garlic-and-cheese naan specifically. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy plain naan (NDB 18960) and ICMR-NIN plain naan baseline, with proportional adjustments for cheese and garlic additions. GI is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain white-flour naan (GI ~71) as the closest defensible analogous food. Confidence is medium rather than high because the composite adaptation introduces estimation uncertainty.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated by adapting plain naan data from USDA SR Legacy (NDB 18960) and ICMR-NIN, with approximately 10–12% mozzarella/processed cheese and ~3% garlic added by weight. This introduces moderate estimation uncertainty. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary source databases used for this composite estimate. 3. vitamin_e_mg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary source databases used for this composite estimate. 4. selenium_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary source databases used for this composite estimate. 5. GI of 71 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain white-flour naan; cheese and garlic additions are not expected to materially lower the GI of the refined-flour base. 6. The input food_name 'Galric N Cheese Naan' contains a typographic error ('Galric' for 'Garlic'); the canonical English name has been corrected to 'Garlic and Cheese Naan'. The original misspelling is preserved in aliases. 7. food_group corrected from input 'Dairy and eggs' to 'Grains and staples' as naan is a bread product; dairy is a secondary ingredient. 8. GL per serving = (71 × 47.5 × 1.20) / 100 = 40.5 g for a 120 g serving.
